reasoning: While the LLM composite score is high, the visual score is low and identifies a specific semantic failure where meaningful status icons were detached from their context and moved to the bottom of the document. This finding is corroborated by an extremely low shingling recall (31%), and since false negatives are 9x more costly, human intervention is required to fix the document's functional integrity.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF on page 3, there are three green checkmark icons positioned next to specific sub-bullets under the 'Infrastructure buildout' section. These are meaningful icons indicating the completion status of tasks. In the HTML output, these icons are missing from the list items where they belong. Instead, a single green checkmark icon appears at the very bottom of the document, disconnected from its context. This makes the status information absent from the relevant content area.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Three green checkmark icons next to bullet points 'Replicate data...', 'Replicate all map...', and 'Replicate geodatabases...' on page 3.
concerns.output_quote: A single green checkmark icon is rendered at the bottom of the page, after the 'Tasks' section, and is missing from the bulleted list.
concerns.location: Tasks section, specifically sub-bullets under Infrastructure buildout (Page 3 of source).

action_items: Relocate the green checkmark icons to their original positions next to the relevant sub-bullets in the 'Infrastructure buildout' section.
Verify that no text content was lost, given the very low shingling recall score.
